title: Checking Exhaust Gas Sensor

figures_detected.description: Temperature gauge showing pointer at middle position between 120 and 270
CONSULT data monitor display showing M/R F/C MNT mode
Diagnostic connector for CONSULT with IGN CHK terminal indicated
ECU unit showing RED L.E.D. location

nodes.text: Start engine and warm it up until water temperature indicator points to the middle of gauge.

Method 1 (CONSULT): 1. See "M/R F/C MNT" in "Data monitor" mode. 2. Run engine at about 2,000 rpm for about 2 minutes under no-load. 3. Maintaining engine at 2,000 rpm under no-load (engine is warmed up sufficiently.), check that the monitor fluctuates between "LEAN" and "RICH" more than 5 times during 10 seconds. RICH → LEAN → RICH → 1 time  2 times LEAN → RICH ......

Method 2 (without CONSULT): 1. Set "Exhaust gas sensor monitor" in the self-diagnostic Mode II. (Refer to section EF & EC.) 2. Run engine at about 2,000 rpm for about 2 minutes under no-load. 3. Maintaining engine at 2,000 rpm under no-load, check to make sure that the RED L.E.D. on the E.C.U. or the check engine light on the instrument panel goes ON and OFF more than 5 times during 10 seconds.
Check and adjustment should be made by referring to IDLE SPEED/IGNITION TIMING/IDLE MIXTURE RATIO INSPECTION in section EF & EC.

tools_referenced.name: CONSULT diagnostic tool
tools_referenced.usage: Reading M/R F/C MNT in Data monitor mode to check exhaust gas sensor fluctuation

cross_references: IDLE SPEED/IGNITION TIMING/IDLE MIXTURE RATIO INSPECTION
